title: To prohibit the implementation of the Approved Resource Management Plan Amendment for the Buffalo, Wyoming Field Office of the Bureau of Land Management.
plain_summary: This bill stops the government from using a new land management plan for federal land around Buffalo, Wyoming. The new plan was created after a court said the government had to study how coal mining affects the environment. The updated plan, released in November 2024, stops any new coal mining leases on this land to reduce pollution, but allows existing coal mines to keep working. This bill would prevent that new plan from being used.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.

sections.title: Section 1 Restriction on Approved Resource Management Plan Amendment for Buffalo, Wyoming Field Office of Bureau of Land Management
sections.text: The Secretary of the Interior cannot put into action, manage, or enforce a specific land management plan change for the Buffalo, Wyoming office of the Bureau of Land Management. This plan change was announced in a government notice published on November twenty seventh, two thousand twenty four. The notice was called Notice of Availability of the Record of Decision and Approved Resource Management Plan Amendment for the Buffalo Field Office, Wyoming and appeared in volume eighty nine of the Federal Register on page ninety three thousand six hundred fifty.
